_readme: OWCS stream/VOD sources for the video computer-vision pipeline.
Each entry points the pipeline at ONE broadcast video for ONE match. It
only says WHERE the video is and WHICH broadcast layout to read it with.
It never contains hero comps and is never used to infer comps.
Comps come only from the video CV (source='cv') or manual corrections
(source='manual'). FACEIT supplies the match/map structure that a video
entry pairs to via 'match' (internal id) or 'faceitMatchId'. If the paired
match is not in the DB yet (run FACEIT ingest first), the entry is skipped
with a message — the pipeline never invents a match from a video source.
Resolution order for the video, most-preferred first:
fixtureFrames  -> a committed folder of pre-extracted PNG frames
(offline/demo/CI; no yt-dlp, no ffmpeg, no network)
localFile      -> a local .mp4 on disk (dev; needs ffmpeg only)
vodUrl         -> a stream/VOD URL fetched with yt-dlp (production)
YouTube VOD sources (platform='youtube') are long broadcast streams and
are sampled BY TIMESTAMP with pipeline/video_ingest.py --source <id>. Only
a ~2s section around each sample offset is fetched, so a 6-hour VOD is
never fully downloaded. Fields: id, title, url, platform, date, region,
notes, enabled, sampleIntervalSeconds, layout. These are ingestion-only
until a later step pairs their frames to a FACEIT match/map.
This file is committed so GitHub Actions can run without secrets.

sources.notes: Self-contained offline demo. Synthetic frames + fixture templates; proves ingest->filter->detect->snapshots without any network or real VOD.
Placeholder production entry. Ingest FACEIT facts for this match first, calibrate a layout + hero templates, then set vodUrl. Skipped until the match exists in the DB and templates are built.

sources.title: OWCS broadcast VOD (AfCXDIMPsLE)
sources.notes: ~6h OWCS stream. Ingestion/frame-extraction only for now; comps and FACEIT pairing come later. Preview with: video_ingest.py --source owcs-afcxdimpsle --dry-run (needs yt-dlp + network). Then sample a window, e.g. --start 1:30:00 --end 1:45:00 --sample-interval 60. Fill in real date once known.

sources.title: OWCS — Crazy Raccoon vs ZETA DIVISION (8C105lNzLAM)
sources.notes: PRIMARY real-VOD calibration target. Reference frame at t=0:06:06 (366s) shows a clean live HUD: Crazy Raccoon (left) vs ZETA DIVISION (right), all 10 hero portraits visible. Test: run_owcs_auto.py --source owcs-8c105lnzlam --start 0:06:00 --end 0:06:40 --every 10 --fast --force. Ingestion/frame-extraction + layout calibration only for now; comps come later via CV evidence + review, never from FACEIT.
